name: Boedo-Ojeda and Canal de Castilla Loop
name_original: Báscones de Ojeda y Alar del Rey — circular desde Herrera de Pisuerga
description: This moderate 32.9-mile touring cycling route from Herrera de Pisuerga explores the Canal de Castilla and scenic Boedo-Ojeda natural area.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Herrera De Pisuerga traverse a landscape shaped by the confluence of the Burejo and Pisuerga rivers, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features the historic Canal de Castilla, providing flat greenways, alongside more varied topography including limestone formations of the Monumento Natural de Las Tuerces. Cyclists can explore lush riparian zones and areas within the Las Loras Geopark, which presents a mix of plains and elevated sections. This environment supports a range of touring cycling experiences, from leisurely rides along waterways to routes with more significant elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Herrera De Pisuerga The most popular touring cycling route is Peña Amaya Loop from Alar del Rey, a 35.1 miles (56.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete, offering views towards the Peña Amaya hill. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alar del Rey by the Canal de Castilla — circular from Melgar de Fernamental, a difficult 43.6 miles (70.1 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, providing a scenic journey through the region's waterways. Local touring cyclists also love the Boedo-Ojeda and Canal de Castilla Loop, a 32.9 miles (53.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including sections along the Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Touring cycling around Herrera De Pisuerga is defined by the historic Canal de Castilla, lush riverine environments, and limestone formations, with routes traversing both flat plains and areas with moderate elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging routes. The routes in Herrera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Herrera De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: There are 11 touring cycling routes around Herrera de Pisuerga, offering diverse experiences from leisurely rides along waterways to more challenging paths through varied terrain.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes around Herrera de Pisuerga cater to various skill levels. You'll find 8 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes. Currently, there are no easy routes listed, but many paths along the Canal de Castilla offer relatively flat sections suitable for less experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1174374170/pena-amaya-loop-from-alar-del-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amaya Loop from Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views towards the Peña Amaya hill, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175214598/boedo-ojeda-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boedo-Ojeda and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the historic Canal de Castilla?
A: Absolutely. The Canal de Castilla is a prominent feature of the region, offering picturesque and flat greenways perfect for cycling. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1378606208/alar-del-rey-by-the-canal-de-castilla-circular-from-melgar-de-fernamental" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alar del Rey by the Canal de Castilla — circular from Melgar de Fernamental&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175214598/boedo-ojeda-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boedo-Ojeda and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; incorporate sections along this historic waterway.

Q: What interesting places can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to explore the region's rich history and natural beauty. You can encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4289247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Rey Aqueduct (Canal de Castilla)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5831684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt;. The broader Las Loras Geopark, which encompasses Herrera de Pisuerga, is known for its geological heritage, megalithic sites, and cave hermitages.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that offer scenic views or explore natural monuments?
A: Yes, the region boasts stunning natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1174374170/pena-amaya-loop-from-alar-del-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amaya Loop from Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt; provides views towards the significant Peña Amaya hill. The Monumento Natural de Las Tuerces, with its unique limestone formations, is also within cycling distance and offers extensive panoramic views.

Q: Are there routes that explore the region's historical or cultural sites?
A: Herrera de Pisuerga and its surroundings are rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170921639/palencian-romanesque-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencian Romanesque Loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the area's Romanesque heritage. You can also explore the medieval layout of Herrera de Pisuerga itself, or visit the Archaeological Classroom to learn about the Roman camp of Pisoraca.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Herrera de Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the combination of historic waterways and natural landscapes.

Q: Where can I rent a bike in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: Herrera de Pisuerga offers active tourism services, including bike rentals. Local companies provide options for guided bike tours and rentals, making it convenient to explore the area even if you don't bring your own bicycle.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are cooler, but some routes, especially along the Canal de Castilla, can still be cycled depending on weather conditions.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options?
A: While specific 'easy' routes are not listed, the flat sections along the Canal de Castilla provide excellent, safe options for families and beginner cyclists. These greenways are generally traffic-free and offer a relaxed pace to enjoy the scenery and historic aqueducts.

Q: Are there any specific towns or settlements worth visiting along the routes?
A: Yes, several charming settlements are located along or near the touring cycling routes. You might pass through places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5227954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Melgar de Fernamental&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6159341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which offer glimpses into local life and history, often with amenities for cyclists.
tips: Herrera de Pisuerga is a city with an outstanding history. From the Roman settlement of Pisoraca to the Visigothic occupation, its past is present in the necropolis and the archaeological remains. In the Middle Ages, it became a walled town with gates and a parish church dedicated to Santa Ana. The old town has a Plaza Mayor with arcades and emblazoned buildings.

Palencia town located in the upper part of the Boedo river dividing the town into two neighborhoods. Its name comes from the times of the Reconquest and repopulation of people from Vasconia or the Kingdom of Navarre in the 9th century.

The power of water, the small town of Alar del Rey is literally surrounded by water. The beginning of the Canal de Castilla, an industrial attempt to boost trade by taking advantage of the power of the river during the 18th century. Today, it is a cultural and sports center with the canal a site of cultural importance.
The Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey is an impressive testimony of 18th century hydraulic engineering, where its locks and aqueducts cross the town.

faqs.question: Is this circular cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' for touring bicycles, requiring good fitness. While sections along the Canal de Castilla are generally easy and flat, other parts of the 53 km loop may present more challenging terrain or slight elevation changes. It's not ideal for absolute beginners but suitable for those with some cycling experience and a reasonable fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. A significant portion follows the towpaths (caminos de sirga) of the Canal de Castilla, which are generally flat and accessible. However, the 'moderate' difficulty rating suggests that other segments, particularly between towns or through natural areas, may include unpaved sections or slightly more demanding conditions.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a blend of historical engineering and natural beauty. Key sights include the impressive 18th-century hydraulic engineering of the Canal de Castilla, with its well-preserved locks and old cereal mills, especially the Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey. The route also passes through Herrera de Pisuerga, a town with a historical urban center, including its Plaza Mayor, and the Church of San Bartolomé of Báscones de Ojeda. The natural scenery includes the Boedo-Ojeda natural area, which is particularly beautiful in early to mid-autumn.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally in early to mid-autumn when the flora along the Canal de Castilla is particularly beautiful. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures for touring cycling in the Palencia region. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car when cycling this route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Herrera de Pisuerga, you can look for parking within the town. Given its status as a cultural interest site and a popular starting point for Canal de Castilla routes, there should be public parking options available. It's advisable to check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other well-known paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino del Besaya: Palencia, the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), and the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte Etapa Alar - Frómista). It also briefly touches upon the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 1.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Herrera de Pisuerga and Alar del Rey, which are part of the circular route, offer options for cafes, restaurants, and pubs. Herrera de Pisuerga, known as the 'crayfish capital,' has a historical urban center where you can find establishments to refuel. Alar del Rey also provides amenities for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on many rural cycling paths in Spain, especially on leash. However, if you plan to enter any specific historical sites or buildings, dogs may not be allowed. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and the towpaths of the Canal de Castilla, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there bike rental options available in Herrera de Pisuerga?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, Herrera de Pisuerga is a popular starting point for cycling routes along the Canal de Castilla. It is likely that you can find bike rental services in or near the town, or in larger nearby cities in Palencia province. It's recommended to check with local tourism offices or search online for 'bike rental Herrera de Pisuerga' prior to your visit.

faqs.question: What makes Herrera de Pisuerga a notable starting point for this tour?
faqs.answer: Herrera de Pisuerga is notable for its historical significance and cultural charm. It's recognized as a Property of Cultural Interest, featuring an arcaded Plaza Mayor, buildings with noble coats of arms, and remnants of a medieval wall. It's also known as the 'capital del cangrejo de río' (crayfish capital) and hosts an annual festival in August, adding a unique local flavor to your cycling experience.
